reviews.review_description: This is a full fledged rally game in vr with good cars good tracks in depth content and cheap to buy  can't ask for more the development team have done a great job

Can you add cockpit driving where we can use our hands to stare with the steering wheel please update this game so you can stare with your steering wheel The game already good it just needs steering wheel support and upgrade the graphics more if you can keep up the good work

I only played the game once after purchasing it because I thought you could grip the steering wheel and turn physically... The joystick turning is something that I do not like personally and wish I knew prior to buying it
Sadly not longer in development. Driving is fun, many cars.

A pretty solid rally simulator. The graphics are simple, but the physics are great. The only downside is the controls — it’s a mobile port, so steering feels too sharp, and there’s no smooth throttle control at all.
Great game but now cannot play as loads to a green screen reporting an error code to report but is all frozen. Have remove and reinstalled. Really was enjoying the concept of this title

Love it!!!!!! Keep it coming guys This is sweet!

                            Pixel 🤙😏

reviews.review_title: Massive room for improvement but fun
reviews.review_description:
I'm a massive vr rally fan so I bought this regardless of reviews. It's your standard mobile game but with vr camera. You drive with the stick and the car auto corrects for over steer and assists with drifts. No animations for hand brake. Game runs pretty poorly given how low rez the graphics are. The main menu is weirdly at an angle so it feels like the tent youre in is collapsing. It has micro transactions that you can play to get around which is nice. There's also a lot of visual glitches around the edges of your vision and pop in for stuff on the track as you're driving.

With some polish and optimization this could be very good and I'll definitely change my review when they add it. Besides all that it is a nice little game they have so far
Better than MotoX but the game isn’t polished enough and the frame rate is a bit low. Graphics are not impressive either of course.
It’s smooth and realistic, but you have to use a joy stick to turn and no actual handbrake.
The gamer is OK at best Multiplayer placeholders, I paid $25 now it's $10 RU dev team. hmmm

reviews.review_title: one of the best racer on quest 2 and 3
reviews.review_description: loving this game and no complains  at all❤️😁🤗

Bought the game and until they bring motion controller support, it's not really playable for me, however I didn't refund because I wanna support the developers as I believe this game has so much potential and can't wait to see them implement the much wanted options 🤞

This is a great mobile port it has potential to be a great standalone vr game . If any way possible add some kind of wheel support !!!! At the very least add motion controls for steering right now it's a great game multiplayer will make it better and a steering wheel will make it the most wanted racer on a quest! Great job so far and I'm excited to see what will come!
Always wanted driving game so opted for this 1 & it’s great well done devs
Love it 100% I,m 67 wish there was more car rally games like it
Idk y u can’t implement the phone version gyro scope into the game so when u grab the wheel it activates the gyro other than that i love all CarX technologies games great work ✊

reviews.review_title: Finally a good driving game for quest 2
reviews.review_description: Seriously enjoyable fun.   You'll smile, laugh and have so much fun.
Great Job.

Would be cool if there were some longer tracks and a multiplayer where you race a whole series where everyone starts at a beginning lobby seeing some sights maybe have a couole fun interactables in the lil campsite. Then one by one, the lobby staggers ontk the first strip. First player that left hopefully arrives before anyone else as long as they werwnt passed while having issues, arrives at the next little waiting area to hang out for a minute, discuss times and track until they stagger out again. Maybe have the option to use bots if nobody else is on

But, these arent nitpicks that hurt the game at all, just stuff I personally would appreciate. Ive always wanted to Rally race and this game is a whole lot of fun getting to taste what its like. Plenty to do. And unlock. The cars available feel exactly how they would in the conditions youre driving. One of my favorites

The game is good, I like the graphics and gameplay. But the license tests suck, as does the unskippable introduction (as everyone knows what a brake is!). The variety of tracks is great, but the maximum length of 3km is unfortunately a bit short

reviews.review_title: Wow! This is loads of fun!
reviews.review_description: The physics used in this are wonderful. You can really feel the drift around corners. I like that it's not a complete sim and not a Nintendo style cartoony game either. This is definitely a pick up and play game that anyone can play, not just race nerds. I'm glad I got it

I used to play Dirt Rally VR years ago on the PSVR and was looking for a similar experience on the Quest 3. I saw this was reduced down to £7.99 and thought I’d give it a try.  I was suitably impressed and am really enjoying working my way through the game.  It has loads of upgradable features on the cars and gives plenty of opportunity to speed through a variety of virtual competitive landscapes. I would say this game is a complete bargain for fans of the genre, or those curious sporty types and comes with a high replayable value. Recommended!

It's not GT7 and not WRC but what this does offer is some mostly arcade gameplay similar to Colin McRae and Sega Rally. The visuals on Quest 3 are clean enough and although the textures and trees are pretty iffy you don't really notice as you're focused on keeping the car on the track.

There is a ton of content and although so far it isn't really required you can tune your car if you wish. The two big issues I have that prevent it from being five stars are this:

1. Literal time you have to wait for the car to be repaired. I suspect this is from the f2p origins of the game but I paid for this game, I shouldn't have this 

2. No feedback at all. I know the Quest headsets lack rumble like PSVR 2 does (which feels awesome on a jump in GT7) but some feedback on the controllers would go a long way.

Overall I love this game and it's perfect for quick gameplay as each stage is about two minutes maximum. It's very accessible and is probably the best racer on Quest.
Car x tech you guys outdid yourselfs with this one , absolutely a 10/10 , can’t wait to see what comes
Very nice

The game, from how it looks and controls (not that motion control wouldn't be nice, challenging as it is), and apparent good amount of content- seems completely worth the low asking price! Though I truly hope there aren't things completely locked behind a paywall, what's here still seems worth $10 to me.

The one thing I've noticed thus far that is annoying is the camera positions that I constantly have to adjust. If I'm looking completely straight forward and hit my recenter button on my controller (due to the racetrack starting me as if I was looking far left and my drivers body being slightly offh-center and either too far ahead of me or my view being almost into the windshield of the car...), my recentering STILL puts me at awkward angles and positions.
Also, in the Garage; I don't know if it's intentional or not (seems very odd if so), but the ENTIRE garage is going downward as if the building was built going down a steep-ish hill and is, again, awkward. No amount of recenting and repositioning helped in the slightest, which made me think it was possibly a very strange intentional design choice...

Hopefully that's the biggest issues I come across (that hopefully get patched) and that there are no solid pay walls, because this is otherwise THE best Quest racing/ car game I've played so far,even if it's in barren company in general (still great though).
Could have been cool. But only steering with the thumbstick is a no-go.. Refund incoming..

Please give us CarX street or CarX highway VR I have quest 3,  it's a sick standalone racing sim.

Thanks.

Great game, awesome physics, and challenging.
Motion controls would send this game to A+ tier!
It's not a bad game, little minor bleeps, but it is still in beta, but I enjoy it very much. I can not wait until it's ironed out a bit more.

reviews.review_title: Truly surprised how good this is!
reviews.review_description:
Ok, downloaded and tested…

It’s way better than I thought it would be! I played stood up and all camera views, I had zero motion sickness, no idea how they’ve overcome that. Controls are ok, would like to change the handbrake (from pulling right thumbstick back, to a button press). 
The first few levels are very easy, the first licence, a piece of cake. I was worried that that was as hard as it was going to get, but it does get more difficult as you progress. 
Only dislikes are that the main in game screen (in the garage area) isn’t level, it’s at a slight slope (but it is meant to be level), it feels like I’m leaning all the time. The other annoying thing is the load screens, they track to head movement, they would be much better if they were stationary. 
Even on the Quest 3, graphics were quite low resolution (especially text). However, these are all things that can be improved. 

Car models are really good, handling is on par with V Rally/Colin McRae. 

I haven’t come across any “In-Game Purchases”, servicing cars does take time, but you can either switch to another car while they repair, or spend in game credits/tokens or use an earned coupon. 
Multiplayer isn’t available yet, but option is there for it when it’s ready. 
Courses/stages are quite short, longest seems to be 3km, but that is kind of expected given that it has to run on the Quest. 

Overall… I am very impressed, certainly won’t be refunding this game. There is room for improvement, but can’t see anything from stopping this game becoming better with time.
The game has in game timers to "repair" your car that requires a premium account. I did not pay 24.99 to get a mobile ftp experience.

The game is almost there to replace the PC VR rally games I am starving to have while away from home, but here are few issues I am having.

The camera is tilted in the garage and not centered.

The camera is jittery and not smooth even in the garage while standing still, it seems like it is trying to scan and adjust constantly the depth sensor.

While on the loading screen, lock the image as now when you move and twist, the image moves and rotates with your vision.

While in game the camera is locked to the car and every single bump and twist shakes and makes me dizzy, a little bit of smoothing or making it detached or locking to the horizon will help.

The physics is not bad but the car turns way to quick and slides too twitchy.

Other than that I am enjoying it way too much and definitely can recommend it in hope the devs will continue to improve it.

reviews.review_title: Best racing game on meta store
reviews.review_description:
A lot of content, good physics, far better than any other racing game meta store has to offer. I would give 5 stars if it had virtual hands and motion controls steering.

Forget the hands, just make the throttle sensitive to how much you press the button. Im at my last championships and having only full power or no power, especially in muscle cars, is making things difficult.

Used it once for half hour and it was fine. Turned it on again and it crashed on load up. Tried again. Crashed on load up. Reinstalled it. Still crashes in load up.

Please help.

This game honestly has a lot of potential and I have no doubt that it will make it to the main store at some point. Unfortunately I refunded the game this is a VR Sim racing game that can only be controlled with an analog stick. That really ruins the immersion. It’s bad enough we can’t get a steering wheel on the quest but at least give us motion controls. I wish the developers luck, but for me, that was a dealbreaker. If they fix this, I would definitely reconsider.
The best Racing Game on the Quest. Great, but please Patch the break and Throttle to sensitive Trigger

Given the low quality of some other app lab driving games I didn’t have much hope for this, however it is fantastic.  There is night driving, different surfaces, different views, pace notes, car upgrades and repair, rain, and several different championship types. 

The performance of the game is great on th Q1, and there is a really noticeable difference with car upgrades and different car types. 

The cockpit view works really well and the pace notes are clear , with additional driving aids available if needed. 

It’s just a shame we’ve had to wait so long for such a great rally game!

Framerate while driving is rock-solid in this title, and it feels very much optimized and more or less like a finished game! Visually this game looks very good, dont judge the videos before you've sat in the seat yourself. Car models look particularly good, and at high speed i dont mind the simple vegetation as it has lesser impact on you than what you think.

There's tons to do, both tracks, championships, challenges and cars.. and the list goes on.
Theres also weather changes, rain or snow and different times of day so a track you’ve driven before at day suddenly becomes alot more challenging at night.

Controls, buttons and sticks only. I wish for a motion controlled steering wheel in the future. But to be honest its kind of relaxing playstyle. Its like you play a flat game but you happen to be sitting in the car. Which totally works fine. 

 My wishlist for updates:
- Tracks vary from 1-8 km in length, i would love more of the longer tracks if possible. Free-ride should have the option to select the longest possible track, not be stuck at 3km

- More flags

- Add replay? (i cant find it)

- Co-driver is static, he’s also looking at a very low textured paper. Give him the slightest idle animation to make him a bit more life like. Maybe he can turn his head to you when you turn your head to the right? Small touches like that sells the experience.

Better cockpit resolution, moving instrument cluster and drivers feet on the pedals moving. at least a wish for future updates, or Quest 3 if the 2 cant handle more stuff.

Can you guys make this game compatible with wheel and pedals ? I know its possible, the game Warplanes has hotas support using only the usb-c port on the headset. THAT would set you guys on the map for sure.

When can we expect multiplayer ? Start a discord server and put out a roadmap. So we eager players can help out, give feedback and know whats coming.

This is an awesome rally game that I need standalone for enjoying VR. Thanks for your work.
Only 2 things can make this game the best:

1)adding “motion” controll=virtual hands with controllers (or handtracking) and move a virtual wheel for feeling a great immersion with it.
🙏Please🤤

2) mode with races with more kilometers, or custom mode that connects several stages together and thus feels a longer and more varied journey (if the quest2 can handle it).

3) Driving on grass/off-road makes driving slow and more skidding.

Thanks

Ahh and 4) bhapfic support for feeling feedback too 😎(feets/vest/arms…)

Extra:
** I don’t find any feedback with controllers.

It would be nice if at least crashes, rollovers or when you fall from a jump you could feel something. Or even when skidding something more subtle. The game moves quickly, it doesn't wait to load and that's why textures load as they go. Some occasional tugging, but in general it feels good.
The game's menus are more designed in "mobile touch", because being in front several times I have pressed down on the right joystick... moving the camera instead of lowering the menu and its list😅. You have to press the pointer and drag. (learning on the fly as the game does not indicate it as an initial guide).
The game is highly addictive.

With motion option and controllers feedback I give the 5 stars😉🤞

Videos can't make it clear all the small details that make this a must on the Quest: great driving and physics model, some actually good graphics, good interior detail, shiny metal exterior, actual glass windshield with dirt, dust, weather conditions, custom races (you don't even need to race), an actual garage with car at scale for inspecting (looking at you, Grid Legends) etc

along with Grid Legends, an essential game for motorsports fans

what I want from updates: longer tracks (3KM is just too short), motion controls (even if just tilt as in GL) and possibly some minor missing detail like your avatar legs actually accelerating and braking... and multiplayer obviously, perhaps even with mobile players.

keep it up, good game. Thanks for bringing to Quest.

reviews.review_title: Why is this not in main store ????
reviews.review_description:
Great game. A very really enjoyable rally game great mechanics realistic feel to the game 
. Worth every penny.

reviews.review_title: The best car racing game on quest2.
reviews.review_description: Nice standalone Rally Game.  Usb-controller support would be nice.

Great game ! Very addictive! 

Can’t wait for a multiplayer option

reviews.review_title: As Close To Dirt Rally VR As We'll Get
reviews.review_description: That's it. The title is the review.
as above

The physics, graphics and content are all there. I only wish there are tracks longer than 3KM added as the update.

Hope even more content will be added in time.

For now it's best standalone VR rally racing game.

It's a full game with carreer mode and car tuning options.
X-Box controller support would be nice though.

Otherwise best racing game on Quest so far.

4 stars for making me feel like I was legit playing Dirt Rally on Quest2! Good graphics, great physics, upgrades to car and unlockables,weather and day/night cycle......but MAN the tracks are so short they are over just as soon as you are getting started. Literally around a minute to complete even on the advanced tracks.  Please add some kind of length to these tracks. Its like using a hot rally car to go up a long driveway.
 EDIT: ok throwing on that fifth star. Even tho I would love to see some 6km maps, this game is still way too fun and runs smoother than 99% of games on the official store. Got to give these guys props. Couldnt stop playing last night. This game is lit

Thank you Devs for an amazing and really enjoyable driving game for the Quest 2. Only this and Grid Legends are worth playing. 

Just like the old V Rally in VR. I particularly like the physics and the snow covered courses. The only thing that I'd like to see now is some circuit courses with a personal best lap ghost to race against - that and possible AI cars to race against at the same time, if courses are wider and longer.
Having said that, I am very grateful for the game as it stands !

The only type of game I was missing on Quest was car rallies. This one is not perfect but is really enjoyable, good work to the dev/s. Hope they can keep improving the game so it moves to the main Store. About improvements I would love to see:

- Better visual overall. I'm super fine with Richard Burns Rally graphics, but better stability, clarity, etc. 
- Longer sections/tracks. And maybe allow modding.
- Wheel support, that would be huge.
- More locations, only 4 locations feels short.

Driving is a big part of gaming and the lack of stand alone games like this is sad but thankfully this exists! A good game very responsive driving experience I own grid legends as well but this one I keep coming back to! The one thing I would like to see is a blue tooth steering wheel system! ( I’m trying to figure out how to make a convection kit for my controllers as I have made my own joysticks before I will eventually but I hope vr blue tooth devices will become available as be hand is only good for some things).
Please Make this game hand tracking because this game is Dank asf

reviews.review_title: The best driving experience on Quest 2
reviews.review_description:
*your Bug Report is broken so Unable to report a bug I found.

I bought this today and been playing it for a good 4 hours! It’s absolutely awesome, sure it needs some tweaks, but it’s a great base to start from.
Lots of choice in races, cars, I love that it’s like GT where you race to earn money to upgrade sections of your cars, and the licenses.
I’m still a little confused about the ticket/token system right now but I’m sure that’ll come to light.

I own grid legends and Driven and now this… I think this is the most fun and has most value of them all.

Keep your eye on my ‘GotchaVR’ YT channel
